rationale: Both candidates independently caught the audio_vae.py:141 shared-config mutation bug, which matches GT's most substantive inline finding (amy-why-3459), with Y giving a slightly more precise fix (copy.deepcopy) and evidence trail. However, X completely misses the serving_speech.py language/dialect-dropping concern that GT's amy-why-3459 raised and LHXuuu had to clarify at length — a notable discussion thread in the ground truth — while Y flags it almost identically, asking for a documenting comment. Both sets of findings are grounded in the diff with concrete file/line references and no fabrication, though Y's systematic 'validated/sweep' evidence annotations give it a slight edge in actionability and verifiability. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
